breaking_news.title: Iran-Linked Handala Hacktivist Group Wipes 200,000+ Devices at Stryker
breaking_news.summary:
Paragraph 1: The Iran-linked hacktivist group Handala (also known as Void Manticore or Handala Hack Team) claimed responsibility for a destructive cyberattack on medical technology giant Stryker, allegedly wiping over 200,000 servers, desktops, and mobile devices globally. The attack, which occurred on March 11, 2026, disrupted operations across 79 countries, forcing thousands of employees to be sent home. Evidence suggests the attackers compromised Microsoft Intune management infrastructure to execute remote device wipes, rather than deploying traditional malware.

Paragraph 2: The incident represents one of the largest wiper attacks in recent history, comparable in scale to the 2012 Shamoon attack on Saudi Aramco. Stryker confirmed the disruption but stated there was no indication of malware or ransomware—only remote wiping via its internal Microsoft environment. The attack, claimed as retaliation for U.S.-Israel strikes on Iran, highlights the growing use of wiper malware by state-aligned actors to cause operational disruption and reputational damage, raising serious concerns for critical infrastructure and medical device manufacturers worldwide.

breaking_news.title: Google Patches Two Actively Exploited Chrome Zero-Days (CVE-2026-3909, CVE-2026-3910)
breaking_news.summary:
Paragraph 1: Google released an out-of-band security update for Chrome on March 13, 2026, patching two high-severity (CVSS 8.8) zero-day vulnerabilities—CVE-2026-3909 in the Skia 2D graphics library and CVE-2026-3910 in the V8 JavaScript engine—that were actively being exploited in the wild. Both flaws require only that a user visit a malicious website to trigger memory corruption, enabling remote code execution. Google discovered both vulnerabilities on March 10, 2026, and confirmed active exploitation before patching.

Paragraph 2: These are the third and fourth actively exploited zero-days in Chrome in 2026, underscoring the increasing targeting of widely deployed browsers by sophisticated threat actors. Given Chrome’s dominance (over 65% global market share), the vulnerabilities pose a severe, widespread risk to individuals and enterprises alike. The low attack complexity and lack of user interaction beyond visiting a page make this a high-priority patch for all users. Organizations must update to Chrome version 146.0.7680.75 or later immediately.

breaking_news.title: Storm-2561 Deploys Fake Enterprise VPN Clients via SEO Poisoning to Steal Credentials
breaking_news.summary:
Paragraph 1: Microsoft Threat Intelligence disclosed that the cybercrime group Storm-2561 has been distributing fake, digitally signed enterprise VPN clients from Cisco, Fortinet, Ivanti, Check Point, and Sophos via search engine optimization (SEO) poisoning. The campaign, active since at least May 2025 but escalating in early 2026, redirects users searching for legitimate VPN software to malicious ZIP files hosted on GitHub and attacker-controlled domains. Once installed, the trojans harvest VPN credentials and session tokens before redirecting users to the genuine vendor download page to avoid suspicion.

Paragraph 2: This supply chain–adjacent attack leverages trusted platforms (GitHub) and legitimate software branding to bypass user skepticism and traditional security controls. The campaign demonstrates evolving phishing tactics where attackers mimic enterprise software distribution to compromise high-value corporate accounts. Microsoft recommends enterprises block suspicious GitHub-hosted downloads, enforce strict software procurement policies, and deploy endpoint detection for credential-stealing processes.

breaking_news.title: Veeam Patches Five Critical RCE Flaws in Backup & Replication (CVSS 9.9)
breaking_news.summary:
Paragraph 1: Veeam released emergency patches for five vulnerabilities in its widely used Backup & Replication platform, including three critical remote code execution (RCE) flaws (CVE-2026-21666, CVE-2026-21667, CVE-2026-21708) with CVSS scores of 9.9. These flaws affect versions up to 12.3.2.4165 and allow authenticated users to execute arbitrary code on backup servers under certain conditions. Backup systems are high-value targets for ransomware operators due to their role in data recovery and potential for lateral movement.

Paragraph 2: The vulnerabilities pose extreme risk to organizations relying on Veeam for backup infrastructure, especially given the increasing frequency of ransomware attacks targeting backup systems to prevent recovery. CISA and security researchers have repeatedly highlighted backup compromise as a key tactic in modern ransomware operations. Organizations must patch immediately and verify integrity of backup data and configurations, as exploitation could lead to data destruction or permanent loss.

breaking_news.title: CISA Adds n8n RCE Bug (CVE-2025-68613) to KEV Catalog Amid Active Exploitation
breaking_news.summary:
Paragraph 1: CISA added a critical remote code execution (RCE) vulnerability in the n8n workflow automation platform (CVE-2025-68613, CVSS 10.0) to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, citing active exploitation in the wild. The flaw, discovered in December 2025, enables unauthenticated attackers to execute arbitrary code via expression injection. With over 57,000 weekly downloads on npm, n8n is widely used for low-code automation across enterprises, often exposed to the internet.

Paragraph 2: The KEV designation mandates patching within 30 days for U.S. federal agencies, but private-sector organizations using n8n must also prioritize remediation. Exploitation could lead to full server compromise, credential theft, and lateral movement into connected cloud and SaaS environments. Given n8n’s integration-heavy architecture (400+ connectors), a single compromised instance could enable widespread data exfiltration. Organizations should isolate n8n instances, enforce authentication, and apply patch build 0.236.0 or later immediately.

breaking_news.title: Authorities Disrupt SocksEscort Proxy Botnet Compromising 369,000+ Devices
breaking_news.summary:
Paragraph 1: Law enforcement agencies from the U.S., Europe, and seven other countries dismantled the SocksEscort malicious proxy service on March 11, 2026, as part of Operation Lightning. The service, powered by the AVrecon botnet, had compromised approximately 369,000 residential routers and IoT devices across 163 countries since 2020. Operators sold access to over 35,000 proxies, enabling cybercriminals to mask their location and conduct large-scale fraud, credential stuffing, and malware distribution.

Paragraph 2: The takedown involved seizing 23 servers, freezing $3.5 million in cryptocurrency, and disconnecting infected devices globally. This operation highlights the growing threat of residential proxy services as infrastructure for cybercrime—and the increasing willingness of international authorities to disrupt them. Organizations should monitor for AVrecon indicators, audit for compromised edge devices, and block known malicious IPs. The disruption may temporarily reduce fraud volume but could prompt threat actors to adopt alternative proxy services.

breaking_news.title: Chinese APT CL-STA-1087 Targets Southeast Asian Militaries with AppleChris & MemFun
breaking_news.summary:
Paragraph 1: A suspected China-based cyber espionage operation, tracked as CL-STA-1087 by Palo Alto Networks Unit 42, has been conducting a long-running campaign (since at least 2020) targeting Southeast Asian military organizations. The group deployed custom backdoors including AppleChris (a macOS/iOS implant) and MemFun (a Windows memory-resident loader) to maintain persistent access and exfiltrate sensitive communications, personnel data, and operational intelligence.

Paragraph 2: This campaign demonstrates strategic patience and high operational sophistication, with attackers using spearphishing, watering holes, and supply chain compromises to infect high-value targets. The targeting of military entities underscores the ongoing use of cyber espionage for strategic advantage in the Indo-Pacific region. Organizations in defense, government, and critical sectors should prioritize detection of AppleChris and MemFun artifacts, enforce network segmentation, and conduct threat hunting for indicators of long-term compromise.

breaking_news.title: Ransomware Incident Responder Accused of Helping BlackCat During Negotiations
breaking_news.summary:
Paragraph 1: The U.S. Department of Justice unsealed charges against an incident responder accused of conducting cyberattacks and simultaneously aiding ransomware gangs—including BlackCat (ALPHV)—during negotiations with the same victims he was contracted to assist. The responder allegedly manipulated negotiations to increase ransom demands and shared victim data with attackers, enabling further exploitation and data exfiltration.

Paragraph 2: This insider threat case represents a disturbing evolution in ransomware operations, where compromised professionals abuse their trusted position to facilitate attacks. It highlights the critical need for rigorous vendor vetting, contract controls, and monitoring of third-party access during incident response. Organizations must ensure incident response agreements include cybersecurity clauses, restrict access to sensitive systems, and mandate transparency in communication with law enforcement.
